bins.name: Technology-neutral flexibility framework
bins.desc: Emerald AI supports defining flexibility in performance-based, technology-neutral terms where any method to verifiably reduce net load qualifies, regardless of whether via software, hardware, or hybrid approaches. This includes recognizing software-defined flexibility as equivalent to generation for grid balancing.
quotes: We align ourselves with the broad coalition of commenters—including the Advanced Energy Management Alliance (AEMA), utilities, transmission owners, and technology companies—in supporting a Flexible Load Fast Track (FLFT).
harness the full suite of flexibility technologies in a technology-neutral and performance-based way.

If a facility can verifiably reduce its net load at the Point of Interconnection (POI) within a specified timeframe, the method used to achieve that reduction should be irrelevant to the transmission provider.
Emerald AI specifically urges the Commission to recognize Software-Defined Flexibility as a valid form of dispatchability and curtailment, equivalent to generation.
A reduction in demand (megawatts off) is physically equivalent to an increase in supply (megawatts on) for the purposes of balancing the system.

bins.name: Cost efficiency over generation mandates
bins.desc: Emerald AI opposes requirements to build matching generation, arguing software-defined flexibility is more cost-effective, can be deployed immediately, and achieves the same grid balancing outcome without capital-intensive multi-year buildouts.
quotes: harness the full suite of flexibility technologies in a technology-neutral and performance-based way.
Requiring physical generation (as suggested by the PJM IMM, Monitoring Analytics) erodes the value of software-defined curtailment and unnecessarily increases costs.

If a facility can verifiably reduce its net load at the Point of Interconnection (POI) within a specified timeframe, the method used to achieve that reduction should be irrelevant to the transmission provider.
Building new generation is capital-intensive and delay projects by several years. Software-defined flexibility can be deployed immediately.
Mandating that data centers build matching generation to receive expedited interconnection is economically inefficient and technologically regressive.

bins.name: Expedited access for flexible loads
bins.desc: Emerald AI supports expedited interconnection pathways for data centers that can verifiably reduce load through software orchestration, without requiring generation buildouts as a precondition.
quotes: Building new generation is capital-intensive and delay projects by several years. Software-defined flexibility can be deployed immediately.
Mandating that data centers build matching generation to receive expedited interconnection is economically inefficient and technologically regressive.

bins.name: Software as grid-interactive asset
bins.desc: Emerald AI contends that software-orchestrated load flexibility (temporal, spatial, and resource-based) should be recognized as a valid, credible, peer-reviewed grid asset for data centers, capable of providing verifiable curtailment and demand response.
quotes: software-defined flexibility of data centers should be eligible as one of many qualifying technologies to provide load flexibility to the power system.
Emerald AI specifically urges the Commission to recognize Software-Defined Flexibility as a valid form of dispatchability and curtailment, equivalent to generation.
Emerald AI has already demonstrated through recent peer-reviewed research (Colangelo et al., Nature Energy, 2025) that AI data centers can provide grid-interactive value reliably.

bins.name: Opposition to generation-only pathway
bins.desc: Emerald AI rejects arguments that large loads must pair with on-site generation for expedited interconnection, calling such requirements economically inefficient and dismissive of modern AI data center operational capabilities.
quotes: Requiring physical generation (as suggested by the PJM IMM, Monitoring Analytics) erodes the value of software-defined curtailment and unnecessarily increases costs.
Mandating that data centers build matching generation to receive expedited interconnection is economically inefficient and technologically regressive.

bins.name: Utility validation of software solutions
bins.desc: Emerald AI notes that transmission owners like National Grid have validated software-defined flexibility approaches, are actively working with companies like Emerald AI on advanced analytics and demand management, and recognize that flexible loads can contribute to cost efficiency.
quotes: National Grid provides direct transmission owner support for this approach, stating they are actively working to implement solutions to make this possible, including through our engagements with companies such as Emerald AI which focus on advanced analytics and flexible demand management
